articles.title: Why Conor vs Holloway Is So Hard to Sell
articles.description: Conor McGregor vs Max Holloway sounds massive on paper. So why does Chael Sonnen think it’s such a difficult fight to explain? Chael breaks down the unusual reality surrounding a possible Conor vs Holloway matchup — a fight without a title, without a grudge, and without the normal ingredients that make blockbuster MMA main events work. According to Chael, big fights almost always need one of two things: a championship… or real personal conflict. This fight has neither. Conor already beat Max years ago. The two don’t dislike each other. Max hasn’t spent years chasing revenge. And now the UFC could be putting them together at a weight class neither guy truly belongs in. So what exactly is the story? Chael dives into why Conor has historically been the perfect promoter’s dream, why McGregor’s trash talk worked differently against Jose Aldo and Floyd Mayweather, and why this may be one of the hardest fights Conor has ever had to “sell.” He also proposes a possible solution involving Michael Chandler — and explains why every great fight ultimately has to lead somewhere meaningful. Because if this fight happens… the UFC still has to answer one question: Why are we here? #UFC #MMA #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #MichaelChandler #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Conor vs Max confusion0:38 How big fights are actually built1:46 Why main events need belts or grudges3:02 The “Money Belt” story4:30 Chael found the Mayweather belt5:52 Why Conor has a difficult job here6:42 Conor already beat Max7:38 Why Conor’s old trash talk worked9:08 Jose Aldo and Mayweather examples10:14 Why Max won’t sell the fight11:18 The weight class problem12:20 What happens if Max wins?13:14 Chael’s Michael Chandler angle14:32 “Why are we here?”15:28 Waiting for Conor’s answer Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Will We See Another Netflix MVP Event?
articles.description: Chael Sonnen thinks Ronda Rousey pulled off one of the smartest promotional performances of her career. Because Ronda vs Gina Carano was never really about Gina Carano. Chael breaks down why Ronda found a completely different angle to sell a fight that had no title, no rankings implications, no personal hatred, and no obvious future attached to it. Instead of pretending she had a grudge with Gina, Ronda turned the story into something bigger — Hunter Campbell, the UFC, MVP, and the idea of competing promotions. Even if none of it was actually real. According to Chael, that’s the entire point of fight promotion. He explains why fans often feel “fooled” when fighters later admit the rivalry wasn’t personal, why personal grudges remain the most powerful force in combat sports, and how fighters can still build compelling stories even when they don’t genuinely dislike each other. Chael also compares the situation to Sean Strickland vs Khamzat Chimaev, explaining why those fighters could still create massive interest without relying on fake hatred. Because in the fight business, the audience doesn’t just buy the matchup. They buy the angle. #UFC #MMA #RondaRousey #GinaCarano #ChaelSonnen #JakePaul 0:00 Ronda’s comment stuck with Chael0:36 “Favorite version” of Ronda1:18 Why the fight was hard to promote2:04 Ronda became the heel2:52 Ronda made it about the UFC3:48 Jake Paul reveals the truth4:42 Why fans feel “fooled”5:34 Ronda’s angle explained6:22 How do you sell fights without hate?7:12 Pereira and Holgate example8:04 Why personal grudges matter most9:06 Sean vs Chimaev comparison10:10 You can sell fights different ways11:02 “They buy the angle” Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Conor’s Comeback Has Never Been Done #ufc
articles.description: Conor McGregor vs. Max Holloway at International Fight Week would be more than just a blockbuster matchup—it would be one of the greatest comeback attempts in MMA history. In this clip, the focus is on the impossible mountain McGregor would have to climb to win. Fighters have returned from devastating leg injuries before, including Anderson Silva and Chris Weidman. They remained dangerous competitors, but none ever fully returned to the level they once reached before the injury. That’s obstacle number one. Obstacle number two is the layoff. No fighter has successfully sat out for six years and returned against a current elite contender at the highest level. And then comes obstacle number three: the weight class. McGregor would reportedly be competing outside his natural division against an active, dangerous opponent still fighting at the top of the sport. That’s what makes this fight so compelling. The challenge isn’t just physical—it’s historical. McGregor wouldn’t simply be trying to win a fight. He’d be attempting something MMA history says shouldn’t be possible. And that’s exactly why fans can’t stop talking about it. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real fight game insight. #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #UFC #MMA #InternationalFightWeek #FightTalk #CombatSports

articles.title: Jon Jones and Francis Could Fight Tonight #ufc
articles.description: Francis Ngannou vs. Jon Jones remains one of the biggest “what if” fights in combat sports—and in this clip, the argument is that nothing is truly stopping it from happening. The discussion dives into why the matchup still captures attention years later while also criticizing Ngannou’s approach to building interest around his fights. The point being made is simple: when fans only care about one possible opponent, that creates a major promotional problem. And that opponent is Jon Jones. The clip also touches on how differently Jones presents himself publicly compared to the larger-than-life aura surrounding the matchup. According to the argument here, Jones and Ngannou could theoretically fight at any time if both sides genuinely wanted it badly enough, regardless of contracts or promotional politics. That’s what makes the situation so fascinating. Because outside of Jon Jones, there may not be another opponent that creates the same excitement for Ngannou. And with heavyweight storylines constantly shifting between MMA and boxing, the pressure to finally make this fight only keeps growing. Fans have wanted this matchup for years—and they still haven’t let it go. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real fight game insight. #JonJones #FrancisNgannou #UFC #MMA #Heavyweight #CombatSports #FightTalk

articles.title: Francis and Jon Don’t Want to Fight
articles.description: Francis Ngannou vs Jon Jones keeps getting talked about. Chael Sonnen thinks that’s exactly the point. Chael breaks down the strange segment involving Francis, Jon Jones, Ariel Helwani, and Jake Paul — and explains why the interview may have been more important than the fight itself. According to Chael, Jon Jones already did everything required to make the Francis fight happen years ago. He vacated the light heavyweight title, spent years bulking up for heavyweight, and fully expected Francis to be waiting for him when he arrived. Instead, Francis left. Now the narrative has somehow shifted into contracts, promotions, and legal complications — but Chael argues none of that is actually stopping the fight from happening if both men truly wanted it. He also explains why the entire segment exposed the difference between being a real promoter and simply being an event planner, why Jake Paul’s presence changed the energy instantly, and why Francis still struggles to understand how to build meaningful fights outside the cage. Because right now, Ngannou faces a difficult reality: The biggest fight available is against a man everyone pretends he can’t fight. And the other option might be his own boss. #UFC #MMA #JonJones #FrancisNgannou #JakePaul #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Francis vs Lins reaction 0:42 Why the segment mattered more than the fight 1:30 Lins actually did his job 2:14 Promoter vs event planner 3:18 Ariel brings Jon Jones into it 4:10 Jon takes the bait 5:02 Jon originally came to heavyweight for Francis 6:08 Jon’s hands are clean 7:00 Francis left when the fight was ready 8:04 “They can fight right now” 9:02 They don’t actually want to fight10:08 Winning public perception matters more11:18 Why Jake Paul changed the segment12:12 Francis doesn’t know how to build fights13:06 Nobody wants to see Francis vs Lins14:02 The real cliffhanger moving forward Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Most Insulting Part of Ronda vs Gina
articles.description: Gina Carano got up smiling. And for Chael Sonnen, that may have been the most revealing moment of the entire night. Chael breaks down the strange emotional dynamic surrounding Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano — not just the fight itself, but the energy, the body language, and the feeling that Gina was genuinely enjoying every second of it. Because there’s something uniquely insulting about watching somebody smile in the middle of chaos. Chael explains why Ronda was put in one of the hardest promotional positions imaginable: no belt, no title, no rankings stakes, no real personal grudge, and a crowd heavily leaning toward Gina from the beginning. Yet somehow, the fight still carried tension. He also dives into the larger Netflix and MVP presentation, why the show exceeded expectations from a production standpoint, and whether this experiment could turn into something much bigger moving forward. But the moment that stuck with Chael wasn’t the production. It wasn’t the commentary team. It wasn’t even the result. It was Gina Carano standing there… smiling. #UFC #MMA #GinaCarano #RondaRousey #ChaelSonnen #JakePaul 0:00 Gina Carano smiling stood out 0:32 Why the fight wasn’t the real story 1:12 Netflix and MVP worked surprisingly well 2:08 Was the event actually successful? 3:20 Why subscriber retention matters 4:08 Chael praises the presentation 5:02 Kenny Florian and Mauro reaction 5:42 Jon Jones, Cain, and Tyron appearances 6:38 Why Gina winning mattered 7:18 Holly Holm and Cyborg possibilities 8:10 Ronda chasing “the fix” 9:08 Will Ronda fight again? 10:00 The myth of Ronda vs Gina 11:02 Why this matchup never truly existed 12:04 Everybody left whole 12:42 “Will there be another event?” 13:18 Gina’s smile changed the feeling of the night Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Dana White Thought Strickland Won #ufc
articles.description: Sean Strickland vs. Khamzat Chimaev may be one of the closest UFC title fights in recent memory—and even now, people still don’t fully agree on who won. In this clip, the focus shifts to the reaction after the fight and why the rematch debate has become so complicated. There are fans who believe Chimaev deserved the decision, but there hasn’t been the overwhelming outrage that normally follows a controversial scorecard. Even UFC CEO Dana White publicly said he scored the fight for Strickland. And inside the arena, the crowd reaction told a similar story. That’s why this creates such an interesting situation for the middleweight division. Strickland’s options now appear to come down to two names: Chimaev or Nassourdine Imavov. One fight is dangerous, emotional, and demands a rematch. The other may offer a cleaner stylistic matchup and a clearer path forward. Because at championship level, the goal isn’t just proving toughness. The goal is maximizing opportunity—biggest paycheck, best matchup, smartest move. And now Strickland has to decide which path he wants. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real fight game insight. #SeanStrickland #KhamzatChimaev #DanaWhite #UFC #MMA #Middleweight #FightTalk

articles.title: Do Strickland and Chimaev Need a Rematch? #ufc
articles.description: Sean Strickland may have earned more respect in defeat than most fighters do in victory. In this clip, the breakdown centers around Strickland’s performance against Khamzat Chimaev and the growing debate over whether the UFC should book an immediate rematch. While some observers favored Chimaev’s forward pressure and aggression, others believed Strickland’s tactical retreat, jab, and defensive awareness were winning the fight round by round. And that’s what made the result so compelling. Strickland never gave up on himself. Even while backing up, he was controlling distance, shutting down wrestling opportunities, and continuing to land shots. Meanwhile, Chimaev showed toughness, pressure, and championship composure in one of the toughest fights of his career. Then came the moment after the fight. The respect. The handshake. The belt placement. It changed the energy completely. Instead of anger or controversy, the ending felt honorable—and that’s part of why the rematch conversation has become so fascinating. Because this wasn’t domination. This was razor thin. Now the UFC has a major decision to make. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real fight game insight. #SeanStrickland #KhamzatChimaev #UFC #MMA #Middleweight #FightTalk #CombatSports

articles.title: The One UFC Fighter Who Actually Wants the Smoke #ufc
articles.description: Alex Pereira may be one of the most dangerous men in MMA, but that didn’t stop Josh Hokit from trying to create a moment. In this clip, the focus is on the chaos surrounding Pereira, Hokit, and Ilia Topuria during a live UFC event setup that suddenly turned tense. The UFC reportedly seated Hokit and Pereira only one seat apart with manager Ali Abdelaziz in the middle, seemingly unaware of the tension already brewing between them. And Hokit leaned directly into it. This wasn’t just about punches and kicks anymore. This was about personality, entertainment, timing, and understanding how to create attention in today’s fight game. Hokit came prepared for Pereira. What he didn’t prepare for was Topuria stepping in and changing the entire dynamic on the fly. That’s where things got interesting. Anybody can plan a confrontation. The real test is whether you can adapt when the moment changes in real time. Hokit already proved he can out-talk people. The bigger question now is whether he can outperform them too. One thing is certain: he got everyone’s attention. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real fight game insight. #AlexPereira #IliaTopuria #UFC #MMA #JoshHokit #FightTalk #CombatSports

articles.title: Conor McGregor Is Taking the Ultimate Test
articles.description: Conor McGregor vs Max Holloway at International Fight Week feels bigger than just another comeback fight. Chael breaks down why this matchup carries enormous stakes for Conor, why the atmosphere in Las Vegas will be unlike anything fans have ever experienced, and why this may be the toughest challenge of McGregor’s entire career. The Irish takeover of Vegas is coming again — and Chael explains why International Fight Week becomes a completely different event when Conor is attached to it. The chants, the crowds, the energy, the chaos… there’s nothing else like it in combat sports. But then comes the reality of the fight itself. Chael points out that Conor isn’t just overcoming one obstacle — he’s trying to overcome three. A catastrophic leg injury that historically nobody has fully recovered from. A six-year layoff against an active contender. And a move to a weight class that was never truly his division. And despite all of that, Conor chose Max Holloway. Not an easy fight. Not a tune-up. One of the most dangerous possible opponents available. This isn’t just a comeback. According to Chael, this is the test of all tests. #UFC #MMA #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #ChaelSonnen #UFCVegas 0:00 Conor vs Max rumors heat up 0:42 “Treat this like Conor’s last fight” 1:32 Fans wanting something new 2:26 Why the matchup feels special 3:12 The Irish takeover of Las Vegas 4:28 Why International Fight Week is different 5:46 Conor and Max at welterweight 6:30 The leg injury reality 7:26 Nobody has ever fully come back 8:10 The six-year layoff problem 9:02 Why active contenders matter 9:44 Conor faces THREE impossible challenges 10:36 Why Max is such a dangerous choice1 1:12 “The test of all tests” Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Francis Still Doesn’t Know How to Sell Fights
articles.description: Francis Ngannou got the win over Phillipe Lins — but Chael Sonnen thinks the fight itself was the least interesting part of the night. Instead, the real story became everything happening around Francis. Chael breaks down the massive size difference between Ngannou and Lins, why the matchup never felt competitive, and why Francis asking for “Fight of the Night” recognition felt strange under the circumstances. Then things get interesting. Jon Jones appeared live at the event, standing cageside in what may have been the most talked-about white T-shirt in MMA history. Francis once again hinted at wanting the Jones fight — but Chael reminds fans that the fight was already lined up years ago… and it was Francis who walked away. So what changed? Chael also explains why contracts are not actually stopping Jon Jones and Francis Ngannou from fighting if both sides truly wanted it, why Ariel Helwani extending the interview mattered, and how Jake Paul’s presence may have quietly opened new possibilities for Francis moving forward. Because right now, Ngannou faces a difficult reality: Fans don’t really know who they want to see him fight next. And that may be the biggest problem of all. #UFC #MMA #FrancisNgannou #JonJones #JakePaul #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Francis vs Lins reaction 0:24 Chael questions the matchup 1:10 The massive size difference 2:06 Jon Jones’ surprise appearance 2:42 The white T-shirt mystery 3:24 Chael calls out the Jones narrative 4:28 Francis walked away the first time 5:16 Islam Topuria comparison 6:00 “They can fight tonight” 7:03 Ariel keeps the interview alive 8:02 Jake Paul changes the conversation 9:06 Francis doesn’t know how to sell fights10:00 What happens next?10:42 Why the segment actually worked Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

Friend of the show Ben Askren joins Daniel Cormier in breaking down the Ronda-Gina fight, and giving their reaction to the rest of the card! The guys are adamant that Francis Ngannou needs a more challenging opponent after knocking out Philipe Lins in the first round, despite being 39 years old. Also, the guys talk about Mike Perry's brutal TKO win vs. Nate Diaz and Nate's vintage post-fight rant after the loss. articles.title: If you care about Mike Perry and Nate Diaz, WATCH this!
articles.description: Mike Perry didn’t just beat Nate Diaz. According to Chael Sonnen, he may have changed the way fighters approach boxing altogether. Chael breaks down the strategy Perry used against Diaz — abandoning the traditional “save energy for later rounds” philosophy and instead burning everything early in an all-out assault. It was reckless, dangerous, and completely against the way boxing is normally fought. And it worked. Chael explains why most fighters secretly spend the first several rounds conserving energy while commentators call it a “feeling out process,” why Mike Tyson warned fighters never to chase knockouts unless they were certain they could get one, and how Perry ignored all of it by forcing chaos immediately. The result? A brutal, violent scrap that felt more like a street fight than a boxing match. Chael also discusses Nate Diaz asking for a rematch, whether Perry vs Jorge Masvidal could be next, and why the fight succeeded because neither fan favorite left damaged beyond repair. This wasn’t polished boxing. This was controlled violence — and fans loved every second of it. #UFC #MMA #MikePerry #NateDiaz #ChaelSonnen #Boxing 0:00 Nate Diaz vs Mike Perry reaction 0:15 Both fighters did their jobs 0:35 The Diaz cardio reputation 1:01 Why fighters “slow play” boxing 1:39 The fake “feeling out process” 2:11 Perry’s unusual strategy 2:35 Mike Tyson’s philosophy 3:05 Chael says boxing may change 3:25 Perry shocked Nate early 3:33 Nate asks for a rematch 3:49 Perry vs Masvidal possibility 4:15 Why the fight worked so well 4:48 “That was a street fight” 4:56 Chael credits Mike Perry

articles.title: Netflix and MVP Pulled This Off
articles.description: Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano may have been the headline — but Chael thinks the bigger story was the event itself. Chael breaks down why the partnership between MVP and Netflix worked far better than many people expected, how the presentation elevated the entire show, and why the production felt polished from top to bottom. From Mauro Ranallo and Kenny Florian on commentary to appearances from Jon Jones, Cain Velasquez, and Tyron Woodley, Chael explains why the pacing and flow made the event feel major league. Then comes the bigger question: what now? Would a Gina Carano upset have opened the door for a Holly Holm fight? Could Ronda still return again after finally getting the “fix” fighters always chase? And was the idea that Ronda vs Gina was a long-awaited dream matchup actually true? Chael also dives into the business side of the event — whether a show like this is sustainable, how streaming platforms evaluate success differently, and why keeping subscribers may matter more than creating new ones. The fight mattered. But the future of the promotion might matter even more. Watch more breakdowns and fight analysis from Chael Sonnen. #UFC #MMA #RondaRousey #GinaCarano #JakePaul #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Ronda vs Gina wasn’t the real story 0:28 Netflix and MVP partnership reaction 1:42 Was the event actually successful? 3:18 Why presentation matters 4:32 Commentary team stood out 5:24 Jon Jones, Cain, and Tyron usage 6:26 What if Gina had won? 7:25 Possible Holly Holm matchup 8:12 Will Ronda fight again? 9:27 Fighters chasing “the fix” 10:28 The myth of Ronda vs Gina 12:00 Why the event still mattered 13:08 Chael’s final verdict on Netflix & MVP Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Francis Ngannnou ADAMANT: "SOMETHING WAS OFF" in knockout loss to Anthony Joshua | Daniel Cormier
articles.description: In the most recent episode of the DC show, Francis Ngannou tells Daniel Cormier that in his BRUTAL KNOCKOUT LOSS to Anthony Joshua something felt off the entire fight week. Francis supposedly arrived the arena 5 HOURS before the fight, while Joshua showed up just an hour and a half before. articles.title: Josh Hokit Might Be UFC Champion Soon
articles.description: Chael Sonnen is back answering fan questions — and this week’s edition of Ask Uncle Chael covers everything from Josh Hokit’s future to the truth behind Khamzat Chimaev’s weight cut controversy. Chael explains why Hokit could become one of the most dangerous heavyweights in MMA, why the UFC rankings system makes no sense, and how Alex Pereira completely changes the heavyweight division just by existing. Then the conversation shifts to Sean Strickland’s all-time middleweight ranking, whether Khamzat’s reported “46-pound weight cut” was physically possible, and why fans completely misunderstand what cutting weight actually means. Chael also tells stories about Jeremy Horn, discusses trash talk in MMA, responds to weed-related questions in typical Chael fashion, and gives his honest thoughts on Jorge Masvidal’s new hairline. As always, Ask Uncle Chael somehow becomes equal parts fight analysis, life advice, and absolute chaos. Watch more breakdowns and submit your questions for the next episode. #UFC #MMA #ChaelSonnen #KhamzatChimaev #SeanStrickland #JoshHokit 0:00 Ask Uncle Chael begins 0:18 Josh Hokit’s UFC future 2:52 Alex Pereira at heavyweight 4:55 UFC rankings make no sense 6:15 Coach Herbo question 7:05 Chael on trash talk 8:18 Jeremy Horn stories 11:08 Sean Strickland all-time ranking 13:12 Chimaev weight cut controversy 18:48 Why fighters don’t blame weight cuts 21:37 Masvidal’s new hairline 23:02 Final thoughts on aging Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Francis Could Become MMA’s Biggest Villain
articles.description: Francis Ngannou may have a bigger opportunity outside the cage than inside it. Chael breaks down why Francis should lean fully into the role of MMA’s “real” heavyweight champion — and why the smartest move might be becoming the villain. It starts with a simple idea: the greatest fighters in combat sports history didn’t wait for people to crown them. They crowned themselves. Using Muhammad Ali and Floyd Mayweather as examples, Chael explains how repetition, confidence, and marketing can shape legacy just as much as wins and losses. Francis already has the aura, the size, and the presence. The question is whether he’s willing to say the part out loud. Should Francis walk out claiming he’s the true heavyweight champion? Should he openly call out Jon Jones and Tom Aspinall? Should he embrace the criticism and become the most obnoxious man in heavyweight MMA? Chael thinks there may be real power in that strategy. Because sometimes the most effective thing a fighter can do… is make people angry. Watch more breakdowns and fight analysis from Chael Sonnen. #UFC #MMA #FrancisNgannou #JonJones #TomAspinall #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Greatest boxer experiment 1:28 Why Muhammad Ali became “the greatest” 3:15 Floyd Mayweather copied the formula 4:18 Chael used it in MMA too 5:24 Francis Ngannou enters the conversation 6:41 The “lineal champion” opportunity 8:12 Why the claim would annoy people 9:46 The Mike Van Arsdale story 11:15 Francis already has the aura 12:26 Call out Jones and Aspinall 13:34 Why Francis should turn heel Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench
